channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Apply for UNITAR Multilateral Diplomacy Programme Traineeship 2026 in Geneva (Field Visits and Online Training)
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The 2026 traineeship opportunity offered by the United Nations Institute for Training and Research under its Multilateral Diplomacy Programme presents an exceptional pathway for students and recent graduates seeking practical experience within the United Nations ecosystem. Based in Geneva, this traineeship is specifically designed to immerse participants in the realities of multilateral diplomacy while equipping […]

The post Apply for UNITAR Multilateral Diplomacy Programme Traineeship 2026 in Geneva (Field Visits and Online Training) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The 2026 traineeship opportunity offered by the United Nations Institute for Training and Research under its Multilateral Diplomacy Programme presents an exceptional pathway for students and recent graduates seeking practical experience within the United Nations ecosystem. Based in Geneva, this traineeship is specifically designed to immerse participants in the realities of multilateral diplomacy while equipping them with professional, organizational, and intercultural competencies.

This article provides a detailed breakdown of the traineeship, including its structure, responsibilities, benefits, eligibility criteria, and application process.

Overview of the Traineeship Opportunity

  • Vacancy Reference: TR/2026/006
  • Position Type: Trainee
  • Number of Positions: 2
  • Location: Geneva, Switzerland
  • Programme Unit: Multilateral Diplomacy Programme
  • Start Date: June 2026 (flexible)
  • Duration: 3 months (extendable to 6 months)
  • Application Deadline: 10 April 2026

The traineeship is structured as a full-time, office-based role requiring participation five days a week. While the placement is unpaid, selected candidates will receive a monthly stipend of 900 CHF to support transportation and incidental expenses.

About the Multilateral Diplomacy Programme

The Multilateral Diplomacy Programme (MDP) is one of UNITAR’s flagship initiatives aimed at strengthening global governance and international cooperation. It works closely with governments, universities, and international organizations to design and implement high-quality training programmes.

MDP’s core activities include:

  • Delivering specialized diplomatic training
  • Developing e-learning courses and digital training tools
  • Providing advisory services to institutions
  • Organizing field-based and virtual training experiences

The programme plays a vital role in advancing multilateralism by fostering dialogue, collaboration, and shared understanding among global actors.

Programme Components: Field Visits and Online Training

A defining feature of this traineeship is its dual focus on both in-person and virtual learning experiences.

Field Visits in Geneva

Field visits typically last between one and two weeks and are primarily scheduled for July and August 2026. These visits offer participants a rare opportunity to engage directly with the international diplomatic community in Geneva.

Participants will experience:

  • Expert-led lectures and policy discussions
  • Interactive training workshops
  • Guided tours of international institutions
  • Visits to UN agencies and multilateral organizations
  • Participation in conferences and networking events

These field visits provide firsthand exposure to how diplomacy operates within one of the world’s most important international hubs.

Online Training Programmes

The online component of the programme runs for two to four weeks and is delivered entirely through UNITAR’s e-learning platform.

These programmes include:

  • Self-paced e-learning modules
  • Live webinars conducted via Zoom
  • Virtual workshops and collaborative sessions
  • Online guided tours of global institutions

This blended learning approach ensures accessibility while maintaining high engagement through interactive digital tools.

Roles and Responsibilities of the Trainee

Under the supervision of an Associate Programme Officer, trainees will play a central role in coordinating and delivering both field visits and online training programmes.

Key responsibilities include:

Programme Coordination and Logistics

  • Supporting the planning and execution of training programmes
  • Managing logistical arrangements before, during, and after events
  • Preparing documentation for partner institutions

Stakeholder Engagement

  • Liaising with universities, organizations, and institutional partners
  • Collecting and organizing participant information
  • Maintaining professional communication with diverse stakeholders

Training Delivery Support

  • Assisting in facilitating both in-person and online sessions
  • Monitoring participant engagement during programmes
  • Supporting live webinars and virtual sessions

Content and Platform Management

  • Managing content on UNITAR’s e-learning systems
  • Creating and refining training materials
  • Contributing to digital outreach and communication strategies

On-Site Support During Field Visits

  • Assisting with participant arrivals in Geneva
  • Accompanying groups during institutional visits
  • Ensuring smooth execution of all scheduled activities

Learning Outcomes and Professional Development

By the end of the traineeship, participants are expected to develop a strong set of professional and interpersonal skills. These include:

  • The ability to translate strategic objectives into actionable plans
  • Enhanced independence and initiative in a professional setting
  • Strong working relationships within the UN system
  • A deeper understanding of multilateral diplomacy and international cooperation
  • Experience working in a multicultural and multilingual environment

This traineeship is particularly valuable for those seeking careers in international organizations, diplomacy, policy-making, or global development.

Why This Traineeship Matters

This traineeship is more than just a professional placement; it is an immersive experience into the workings of international diplomacy. Participants gain direct exposure to global governance processes, develop critical skills, and build networks that can shape their future careers.

For individuals passionate about international relations and multilateral cooperation, this opportunity offers a unique combination of practical experience, professional growth, and global engagement

Eligibility Criteria and Requirements

Academic Qualifications

Applicants must meet one of the following criteria:

  • Currently enrolled in an undergraduate, graduate, or postgraduate programme
  • Recent graduate (must begin traineeship within two years of graduation)

Relevant fields of study include:

  • International Relations
  • Political Science
  • Law
  • Education
  • History
  • Languages or related disciplines

Required Skills and Competencies

Successful candidates are expected to demonstrate:

  • Strong organizational and time-management skills
  • Experience in planning events or coordinating field visits
  • Familiarity with online training platforms or digital learning environments
  • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills
  • Knowledge of international affairs and the UN system
  • Commitment to UN values such as cooperation, respect, and solidarity

Additional Assets

While not mandatory, the following will strengthen an application:

  • Experience in strategic marketing and partnership development
  • Skills in digital content creation for social media
  • Existing networks with potential partners or participants

Language Requirements

  • Fluency in English (written and spoken) is mandatory
  • Knowledge of French and/or Mandarin Chinese is considered an advantage

Financial Support and Conditions

Although the traineeship is officially unpaid, UNITAR provides a monthly stipend of 900 CHF. This is intended to help cover transportation and miscellaneous living expenses in Geneva.

Candidates should note that Geneva is one of the most expensive cities globally, and additional financial planning may be required.

Application Process

Applicants are encouraged to submit their materials as early as possible due to the competitive nature of the programme.

Required Documents

  • Completed application
  • Motivation letter outlining interest and suitability

Important Notes

  • Applications must be submitted before 10 April 2026
  • Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted
  • Early application is strongly recommended due to high application volume

The UNITAR Multilateral Diplomacy Programme Traineeship 2026 stands out as a highly competitive and prestigious opportunity for emerging professionals. With its blend of field visits, digital learning, and hands-on coordination experience, it provides a comprehensive introduction to the world of international diplomacy.

Prospective applicants should carefully review the requirements, prepare a strong motivation letter, and apply well before the deadline to maximize their chances of selection.

Discover more similar opportunities here.

The post Apply for UNITAR Multilateral Diplomacy Programme Traineeship 2026 in Geneva (Field Visits and Online Training) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Chatham House Academy Fellowships 2026–2027 in London: Fully Funded 10-Month Global Policy Fellowship with £2,565 Monthly Stipend
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Chatham House Academy Fellowships 2026–2027 offer a prestigious and fully funded opportunity for early- to mid-career professionals to undertake advanced research and leadership development at one of the world’s leading policy institutes in London, United Kingdom. Hosted by Chatham House (The Royal Institute of International Affairs), this highly competitive programme is part of the […]

The post Chatham House Academy Fellowships 2026–2027 in London: Fully Funded 10-Month Global Policy Fellowship with £2,565 Monthly Stipend appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Chatham House Academy Fellowships 2026–2027 offer a prestigious and fully funded opportunity for early- to mid-career professionals to undertake advanced research and leadership development at one of the world’s leading policy institutes in London, United Kingdom.

Hosted by Chatham House (The Royal Institute of International Affairs), this highly competitive programme is part of the Queen Elizabeth II Academy for Leadership and the Next Generation, designed to equip emerging leaders with the knowledge, networks, and skills needed to address global challenges.

Selected fellows will engage in independent research, policy engagement, and leadership training, while contributing to real-world solutions in international affairs.

About the Chatham House Academy Fellowships

The Academy Fellowship is a 10-month, full-time programme beginning in October 2026, combining research, leadership development, and policy engagement.

Participants will:

  • Conduct independent research aligned with Chatham House programmes
  • Contribute to policy discussions, publications, and events
  • Engage with a global network of experts, policymakers, and practitioners
  • Participate in a Leadership Masterclass Programme

The fellowship is designed to develop future global leaders in policy, research, and international affairs.

Types of Fellowships Available

There are four distinct fellowship tracks, each with specific eligibility criteria:

  1. Mo Ibrahim Foundation Academy Fellowship
    • Open to citizens of African countries
  2. New Generation Europe Academy Fellowship
    • Open to Russian citizens or dual citizens
  3. Richard and Susan Hayden Academy Fellowship
    • Open to applicants from any country
  4. Stavros Niarchos Foundation Academy Fellowship
    • Open to Greek citizens

Research Focus Areas

Applicants must align their research proposal with one of Chatham House’s thematic programmes, including:

  • Africa Programme and Digital Society
  • Environment and Society
  • Europe Programme
  • Global Economy and Finance
  • Global Health
  • International Law
  • International Security
  • Latin America Initiative
  • Middle East and North Africa
  • Russia and Eurasia
  • UK in the World
  • US and North America

Each area addresses critical global issues, including climate change, AI governance, geopolitical competition, global health security, and economic transformation.

Fellowship Benefits

The programme offers a comprehensive financial and professional package:

Financial Support

  • Monthly stipend of £2,565 to cover living expenses in London
  • Coverage of:
    • Travel and relocation costs
    • Visa fees and sponsorship
    • UK Immigration Health Surcharge
    • Research-related expenses

Professional Development

  • Participation in the Leadership Masterclass Programme
  • Access to:
    • Leadership workshops
    • Media training
    • Writing and research support
    • Career coaching and mentoring

Networking Opportunities

  • Full Chatham House membership
  • Access to global policy networks
  • Continued 5-year membership after completion

Programme Structure

The fellowship follows a hybrid model:

  • Minimum of two days per week in-office in London
  • Additional in-person engagement required for leadership sessions
  • Full-time commitment required throughout the programme

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Hold a Bachelor’s degree or equivalent
  • Have experience in independent research
  • Demonstrate:
    • Strong academic or professional background
    • Leadership potential
    • Commitment to global policy impact
  • Meet the specific eligibility criteria of the chosen fellowship track
  • Be proficient in English

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  • Completed online application form
  • Updated CV
  • Research proposal aligned with Chatham House priorities
  • Responses to application questions (research plan, methodology, impact, etc.)

Application Deadline

  • Deadline: 7 April 2026 (5:00 PM GMT)

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and early submission is strongly encouraged. If you require assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, please contact academy@chathamhouse.org.

Why You Should Apply

The Chatham House Academy Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to:

  • Conduct impactful research on global policy challenges
  • Develop leadership skills through structured training
  • Engage with influential global networks
  • Enhance your career in policy, research, and international affairs
  • Contribute to building a sustainable, secure, and just world

How to Apply

Learn more and apply through the official platform HERE.

Ensure your application demonstrates clear research focus, leadership potential, and alignment with Chatham House’s mission.

Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Chatham House Academy Fellowships 2026–2027 in London: Fully Funded 10-Month Global Policy Fellowship with £2,565 Monthly Stipend appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded Summer School in Digital Human Rights 2026 at Lund University, Sweden (Free Tuition + Accommodation)
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Summer School in Digital Human Rights 2026 at Lund University offers a prestigious and fully funded opportunity for students, researchers, and professionals to gain advanced knowledge in human rights law in the digital age. Hosted by the Faculty of Law in collaboration with the Raoul Wallenberg Institute, this intensive residential programme will take place […]

The post Fully Funded Summer School in Digital Human Rights 2026 at Lund University, Sweden (Free Tuition + Accommodation) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Summer School in Digital Human Rights 2026 at Lund University offers a prestigious and fully funded opportunity for students, researchers, and professionals to gain advanced knowledge in human rights law in the digital age. Hosted by the Faculty of Law in collaboration with the Raoul Wallenberg Institute, this intensive residential programme will take place in Lund, Sweden from 22–26 June 2026.

This highly competitive programme is designed for individuals seeking to deepen their understanding of how technology, artificial intelligence, and digital systems intersect with human rights frameworks in an increasingly complex global landscape.

Overview of the Programme

The Summer School is a short-term, high-impact academic training that combines expert-led lectures, interactive seminars, and practical discussions with leading professionals in the field.

Participants will engage in critical discussions around:

  • The evolving role of human rights in digital environments
  • The legal and ethical implications of artificial intelligence (AI)
  • The challenges of cybersecurity, surveillance, and digital governance

This programme is particularly relevant in today’s world where digital technologies increasingly shape freedom, privacy, democracy, and global security.

Key Topics Covered

Participants will explore a wide range of cutting-edge issues, including:

  1. Privacy in the Digital Age
    • Data protection, surveillance, and privacy rights
  2. Freedom of Expression Online
    • Social media regulation and digital speech
  3. Business and Human Rights in Cyberspace
    • Corporate responsibility in digital environments
  4. Artificial Intelligence and Human Rights
    • Regulation of AI systems
    • AI manipulation and cognitive freedoms
  5. Cybersecurity and Democracy
    • Electoral interference and hybrid threats
  6. International Cybercrime Law
    • Treaties and their implications for human rights

Programme Structure and Learning Experience

The Summer School is structured to provide both theoretical knowledge and practical engagement:

  • Interactive seminars led by global experts
  • Group exercises and case-based discussions
  • Conversations with practitioners working in digital human rights
  • Exposure to real-world challenges and policy debates

Participants will benefit from a collaborative learning environment, engaging with peers from diverse academic and professional backgrounds.

What the Programme Offers

The Summer School provides substantial financial and academic benefits:

Financial Support

  • No tuition fee (fully funded programme)
  • Free accommodation for six nights in Lund

Additional Support

  • Limited travel grants available (based on need)
  • Invitation letter for visa application support

Academic and Professional Value

  • Access to world-class faculty and experts
  • Networking with professionals and researchers
  • Exposure to cutting-edge global issues in digital rights

Eligibility Criteria

This is an advanced-level programme, and applicants must demonstrate strong academic or professional background in relevant fields.

Applicants must meet one of the following:

  1. Academic Route
    • Hold a Master’s degree in law, politics, international relations, or related fields
    • OR currently be enrolled in a Master’s programme with relevant knowledge
  2. Professional Route
    • Hold a Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field
    • AND have at least 2 years of relevant work experience

Additional Requirements

  • Strong knowledge of human rights law and public international law
  • Excellent English proficiency

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit the following:

  • Statement of interest (maximum 400 words)
  • One-page CV
  • Optional: Travel grant statement (maximum 200 words, if applicable)

Applications exceeding the word limits will not be considered.

Important Dates

  • Applications Open: 20 March 2026
  • Application Deadline: 15 April 2026
  • Programme Dates: 22–26 June 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early due to the competitive nature of the programme.

Why You Should Apply

This Summer School presents a unique opportunity to:

  • Gain specialized knowledge in digital human rights
  • Learn directly from leading global experts
  • Expand your academic and professional network
  • Engage with real-world issues at the intersection of law, technology, and society
  • Enhance your profile for careers in law, policy, advocacy, and international development

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply through the official platform.

Click HERE to apply!

Ensure that all required documents are properly formatted and submitted before the deadline.

Learn more about the Summer School in Digital Human Rights 2026 at Lund University HERE.

Discover more global Scholarship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Fully Funded Summer School in Digital Human Rights 2026 at Lund University, Sweden (Free Tuition + Accommodation) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded IFE Call for Proposals 2026: Grants for Job Creation and Skills Development in Ghana, Senegal & Tunisia
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Facility Investing for Employment (IFE) Call for Proposals 2026 is now open, offering a major funding opportunity for organizations and businesses to implement impactful projects focused on job creation and skills development in Ghana, Senegal, and Tunisia. Launched under the German government’s Special Initiative “Decent Work for a Just Transition”, this programme provides grant […]

The post Fully Funded IFE Call for Proposals 2026: Grants for Job Creation and Skills Development in Ghana, Senegal & Tunisia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Facility Investing for Employment (IFE) Call for Proposals 2026 is now open, offering a major funding opportunity for organizations and businesses to implement impactful projects focused on job creation and skills development in Ghana, Senegal, and Tunisia.

Launched under the German government’s Special Initiative “Decent Work for a Just Transition”, this programme provides grant funding for private sector projects that demonstrate strong potential to create sustainable employment and improve workforce skills.

This initiative is particularly important for organizations aiming to drive economic growth, youth employment, and workforce development in Africa while aligning with global development goals.

About the Facility Investing for Employment (IFE)

The Facility Investing for Employment (IFE) is implemented by KfW Development Bank on behalf of the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ).

The primary objective of the programme is to:

  • Create new, sustainable jobs in the private sector
  • Improve working conditions and income levels
  • Enhance skills development and vocational training
  • Promote inclusive economic growth, especially for women and youth

Projects are evaluated not only on job creation but also on quality, sustainability, and long-term impact.

Key Focus Areas of the 2026 Call

The 2026 call emphasizes:

1. Job Creation (Mandatory Component)

Projects must:

  • Be mature and ready for implementation
  • Demonstrate clear potential for job creation in the private sector
  • Be financially viable and sustainable
  • Include co-financing from applicants

Projects must fall under one of IFE’s defined investment categories.

2. Training and Skills Development (Optional Component)

Applicants can also apply for additional funding to support:

  • Vocational training programmes
  • Workforce upskilling initiatives
  • Language and technical training aligned with labor market needs

Target sectors include:

  • Construction and related industries
  • Metal and electrical industries
  • Education and social services
  • Food processing
  • Transport and logistics
  • Information technology
  • Hospitality and tourism

Eligible activities may include:

  • Building or upgrading training facilities
  • Providing equipment and learning materials
  • Delivering specialized technical or language training

More information can be found in the Guidelines for Applicants that are available in the Download Center.

What the Grant Covers

The IFE programme provides grant funding to support:

  • Capital investments for job-creating projects
  • Infrastructure development
  • Training and capacity-building initiatives

The funding is designed to complement applicants’ own financial contributions, ensuring project sustainability.

Evaluation Criteria

Projects are assessed based on:

  1. Number of new jobs created
  2. Cost per job created
  3. Improvement in working conditions or income
  4. Number of people benefiting from training
  5. Additional impact for women and youth
  6. Contribution to feminist development and just transition
  7. Synergies with other development initiatives

Projects that demonstrate high impact and inclusivity receive additional evaluation points.

Who Can Apply

The programme is open to:

Individual Applicants

  • Legal entities registered in Ghana, Senegal, or Tunisia
  • Must have been operational for at least three years

Consortia

  • Two or more legal entities
  • Must be registered in:
    • The target country
    • Africa
    • EU/EFTA countries

Applicants must demonstrate the capacity to implement and co-finance projects.

Application Process

The IFE Call for Proposals follows a two-stage competitive process:

Stage 1: Concept Note Submission

  • Applicants submit a concept note online
  • Proposals are evaluated based on predefined criteria
  • Top proposals are shortlisted

Stage 2: Full Proposal Submission

  • Shortlisted applicants submit detailed proposals
  • Projects undergo due diligence and potential site visits
  • Approved projects receive grant agreements

Important Dates

  • Application Window Opens: 20 March 2026
  • Application Deadline: 1 June 2026 (17:00 CEST)
  • Questions Deadline: 15 May 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit early and participate in webinars for guidance.

Webinars and Support

To assist applicants, the programme offers:

  • Multiple webinars (April–May 2026)
  • Guidance on application requirements
  • Live demonstrations of the application platform

Applicants can also:

  • Contact the IFE Helpdesk
  • Email support teams for clarification

Why You Should Apply

The IFE Call for Proposals 2026 offers a unique opportunity to:

  • Access significant grant funding
  • Scale projects that create real employment opportunities
  • Improve skills development and workforce readiness
  • Contribute to economic transformation in Africa
  • Align with international development priorities

This is an ideal opportunity for organizations committed to impact-driven development and sustainable job creation.

How to Apply

For projects in Ghana, Senegal and Tunisia, submit your applicationHERE.

Ensure that your concept note is complete and aligned with the programme’s objectives.

In case of questions, please visit the FAQ. If you cannot find the information you need, please send an email to cfp-ife.2026@invest-for-jobs.com

Click HERE to read more about the Facility Investing for Employment (IFE) Call for Proposals 2026.

Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Fully Funded IFE Call for Proposals 2026: Grants for Job Creation and Skills Development in Ghana, Senegal & Tunisia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: UN International Law Seminar 2026 (ILS) at Geneva for Advanced Legal Professionals
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The 60th Session of the International Law Seminar (ILS) will take place from 29 June to 17 July 2026, offering a unique and highly competitive opportunity for individuals deeply engaged in the field of international law. Organized annually by the United Nations Office at Geneva since 1965, this three-week seminar is held in parallel with […]

The post Apply Now: UN International Law Seminar 2026 (ILS) at Geneva for Advanced Legal Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The 60th Session of the International Law Seminar (ILS) will take place from 29 June to 17 July 2026, offering a unique and highly competitive opportunity for individuals deeply engaged in the field of international law. Organized annually by the United Nations Office at Geneva since 1965, this three-week seminar is held in parallel with the sessions of the International Law Commission, making it one of the most prestigious academic and professional programs in global legal studies.

The International Law Seminar stands out as a high-level platform that brings together emerging legal minds and seasoned professionals to explore the development, interpretation, and application of international law in a real-world, diplomatic setting.

About the International Law Seminar (ILS)

The ILS is not a general or introductory course. Instead, it is an advanced-level seminar designed for individuals who already possess a solid foundation in international law. Its primary objective is to deepen participants’ understanding of the work carried out by the International Law Commission—the UN body responsible for the codification and progressive development of international law.

Participants will gain first-hand exposure to the processes through which international legal frameworks are debated, refined, and formalized. This immersive experience allows attendees to observe and engage with some of the most pressing legal issues facing the international community today.

Who Should Apply

The seminar is tailored for a highly specialized audience, including:

  • Postgraduate students pursuing advanced degrees in international law
  • Early-career academics and researchers in legal studies
  • Government officials involved in legal or diplomatic work
  • Young practitioners actively working in international law or related fields

Applicants are expected to demonstrate strong academic credentials or significant professional experience in international law. This ensures that all participants can contribute meaningfully to discussions and benefit fully from the advanced content delivered during the seminar.

Program Structure and Learning Experience

The International Law Seminar runs for three weeks and is conducted alongside the annual session of the International Law Commission. The structure of the program is both intensive and interactive, combining observational learning with active participation.

Key Components of the Seminar

1. Attendance at ILC Sessions
Participants will attend meetings of the International Law Commission, providing direct insight into how international legal norms are shaped and debated at the highest level.

2. Expert Lectures
A series of lectures will be delivered by:

  • Members of the International Law Commission
  • Officials from the United Nations system
  • Representatives from specialized agencies
  • Distinguished guest speakers with expertise in international law

These lectures cover a wide range of contemporary legal issues, offering both theoretical perspectives and practical insights.

3. Interactive Discussions
Following each lecture, participants are expected to actively engage in discussions. This interactive format encourages critical thinking, debate, and the exchange of ideas among participants from diverse legal systems and cultural backgrounds.

A Global Platform for Legal Exchange

One of the defining features of the ILS is its international and multicultural environment. Each year, only 25 participants are selected from across United Nations Member States. This selective process ensures a diverse cohort, fostering meaningful dialogue and collaboration among individuals representing different legal traditions and perspectives.

Participants not only learn from experts but also from one another, making the seminar a truly global forum for the exchange of ideas in international law.

Why the ILS Matters

The International Law Seminar is more than just an academic program—it is a career-defining opportunity. By participating in the seminar, attendees gain:

  • Direct exposure to the workings of the United Nations legal system
  • A deeper understanding of the codification and development of international law
  • Access to a global network of legal professionals and scholars
  • Enhanced credibility and recognition within the field of international law

The experience is particularly valuable for those aspiring to careers in international organizations, academia, diplomacy, or transnational legal practice.

Important Dates

  • Seminar Dates: 29 June – 17 July 2026
  • Session: 60th International Law Seminar (ILS)
  • Deadline is 17th April 2026

How to Apply

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply for the 2026 session of the International Law Seminar through the official application channels provided by the United Nations Office at Geneva. Given the highly competitive nature of the program, applicants should ensure that their academic background and professional experience clearly demonstrate expertise in international law.

The International Law Seminar at the United Nations Office at Geneva remains one of the most prestigious opportunities for advanced learners and professionals in international law. With its rigorous academic framework, direct engagement with the International Law Commission, and diverse global participation, the ILS offers an unparalleled experience for those seeking to deepen their understanding and influence in the field of international law.

For individuals committed to shaping the future of international legal systems, the 2026 session presents a rare and valuable opportunity.

Discover other similar opportunities here

The post Apply Now: UN International Law Seminar 2026 (ILS) at Geneva for Advanced Legal Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Program Manager – Digital Policy & Innovation at ACET: Lead Africa’s Digital Transformation
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Program Manager – Digital Policy & Innovations role at the African Center for Economic Transformation (ACET). This is a high-impact opportunity for professionals who want to shape Africa’s future through digital policy, innovation, and strategic collaboration. About the Role In this role, you’ll work at the intersection of policy, […]

The post Program Manager – Digital Policy & Innovation at ACET: Lead Africa’s Digital Transformation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Program Manager – Digital Policy & Innovations role at the African Center for Economic Transformation (ACET).

This is a high-impact opportunity for professionals who want to shape Africa’s future through digital policy, innovation, and strategic collaboration.

About the Role

In this role, you’ll work at the intersection of policy, technology, and development, helping to design and implement initiatives that drive digital transformation across African countries.

You will collaborate with global partners, governments, and stakeholders to advance African-led solutions in areas such as:

Digital policy and governance
Innovation ecosystems
Green industrialization
Economic transformation

Key Responsibilities

Lead and shape high-level strategy in digital policy and innovation
Collaborate with international and regional partners
Support the design and implementation of cross-country programmes
Contribute to research, policy dialogue, and stakeholder engagement
Drive African-led digital solutions across multiple sectors

Why This Role Matters

Africa’s transformation requires bold, systems-level thinking. This role places you at the center of efforts to shape policies that will influence the continent’s digital future, economic growth, and global positioning.

You’ll be contributing to real change at a strategic level—working on solutions that can scale across countries and impact millions of people.

Application Details

📅 Deadline: Thursday, 2nd April, 2026

🔗 Apply through this link.

For more Job opportunities from OFY, click here.

The post Program Manager – Digital Policy & Innovation at ACET: Lead Africa’s Digital Transformation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: World Food Forum Innovation Awards 2026: Win $17,000, Global Exposure & Pitch at FAO HQ
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the World Food Forum Innovation Awards 2026 (SIA)—a global opportunity for youth-led agrifood startups to showcase their innovations and scale their impact. Organized under the World Food Forum, an initiative connected to the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO), this award supports young innovators tackling challenges in food systems, climate, and […]

The post World Food Forum Innovation Awards 2026: Win $17,000, Global Exposure & Pitch at FAO HQ appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the World Food Forum Innovation Awards 2026 (SIA)—a global opportunity for youth-led agrifood startups to showcase their innovations and scale their impact.

Organized under the World Food Forum, an initiative connected to the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO), this award supports young innovators tackling challenges in food systems, climate, and nutrition.

If you’re building solutions in foodtech, agritech, climate, or nutrition, this is a powerful platform to gain recognition, funding, and global exposure.

About the Innovation Awards

The World Food Forum Innovation Awards are designed to identify and support promising youth-led startups working across agrifood systems.

The programme provides not only funding but also access to mentorship, global networks, and acceleration opportunities to help startups scale their solutions.

Finalists will have the chance to pitch their ideas at FAO Headquarters in Rome, connecting directly with global leaders, investors, and policymakers.

What You Can Win

Selected startups will receive a combination of financial support and strategic opportunities.

Benefits include:

Up to $17,000 in funding
Global visibility and recognition
Mentorship from industry experts
Access to MassChallenge Switzerland’s Accelerator
Opportunity to pitch at FAO Headquarters in Rome

This combination of funding and exposure makes it a highly competitive and valuable opportunity for early-stage startups.

Focus Areas

The awards are open to startups working across key sectors, including:

Agrifood systems
Food technology
Climate and sustainability
Nutrition and food security

Innovative solutions that address real-world challenges and contribute to sustainable food systems are especially encouraged.

Why You Should Apply

This programme is ideal for startups looking to scale their impact and connect with global stakeholders.

By participating, you gain access to resources, networks, and platforms that can significantly accelerate your growth.

Application Deadline

Applications close on April 1, 2026.

Early application is strongly encouraged due to the competitive nature of the programme.

Click Here to Apply

For more Award opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post World Food Forum Innovation Awards 2026: Win $17,000, Global Exposure & Pitch at FAO HQ appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Women in Tech Accelerator Ghana 2026: Grants, Mini MBA & Business Support for Women Entrepreneurs
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Women in Tech Accelerator Ghana, a 6-month programme designed to support women-led businesses that leverage technology to scale, innovate, and drive impact. This initiative is led by Standard Chartered Bank Ghana in partnership with the Ghana Climate Innovation Centre (GCIC), an institute of Ashesi University. The programme is part […]

The post Women in Tech Accelerator Ghana 2026: Grants, Mini MBA & Business Support for Women Entrepreneurs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Women in Tech Accelerator Ghana, a 6-month programme designed to support women-led businesses that leverage technology to scale, innovate, and drive impact.

This initiative is led by Standard Chartered Bank Ghana in partnership with the Ghana Climate Innovation Centre (GCIC), an institute of Ashesi University.

The programme is part of a broader international effort already running in countries such as Kenya, Nigeria, UAE, and Pakistan, aimed at increasing diversity and innovation in the tech ecosystem.

About the Women in Tech Accelerator

The Women in Tech Accelerator is designed to empower women entrepreneurs by providing structured business support, mentorship, and funding opportunities.

It focuses on helping women-led businesses that use technology in their operations to build sustainable, scalable, and impactful ventures.

Participants will benefit from a combination of training, advisory services, and financial support to strengthen their businesses and improve long-term growth outcomes.

What the Programme Offers

The accelerator provides a comprehensive support system tailored to the needs of women entrepreneurs.

Key components include:

A High-Value Mini MBA Programme focused on business growth and strategy
The Women Entrepreneurs’ Transformation Programme for leadership and enterprise development
Opportunities to pitch and secure financial grants to scale operations
Access to dedicated portfolio managers for personalized business support

The programme is structured to ensure participants gain both theoretical knowledge and practical tools to grow their ventures.

Programme Duration

The Women in Tech Accelerator runs for 6 months, offering an intensive and transformative experience for selected participants.

Why This Programme Matters

Women entrepreneurs often face barriers such as limited access to funding, networks, and business training. This accelerator addresses these challenges by providing targeted support to help women thrive in the technology space.

By fostering innovation and entrepreneurial excellence, the programme contributes to economic growth, climate resilience, and inclusive development in Ghana and beyond.

About the Partners

Standard Chartered Bank Ghana is one of Ghana’s leading financial institutions with a long-standing history and global presence across multiple markets.

The Ghana Climate Innovation Centre (GCIC) is a leading business incubator focused on supporting climate-smart enterprises and sustainable innovation in Ghana.

Together, these organizations bring expertise in finance, business development, and sustainability to support the next generation of women entrepreneurs.

Who Should Apply

This programme is ideal for women entrepreneurs who:

Run or lead technology-driven businesses
Are looking to scale and improve their operations
Want access to funding, mentorship, and business training
Are committed to innovation and sustainable growth

How to Apply

Interested applicants should visit the official platforms of Standard Chartered Bank Ghana or the Ghana Climate Innovation Centre for application details and deadlines.

For More Fellowship Opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post Women in Tech Accelerator Ghana 2026: Grants, Mini MBA & Business Support for Women Entrepreneurs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Powerful OIDP Conference 2026 Call for Proposals on Participatory Democracy
item.dc:creator: Michelle Zyongwe
item.description:
The Transformative OIDP Conference 2026 Call for Proposals presents a powerful opportunity for academics, policymakers, civil society leaders, and practitioners to contribute to one of the most important global discussions on the future of democracy. Organized by the International Observatory on Participatory Democracy (OIDP) in collaboration with the Municipality of Kraków and United Cities […]

The post Powerful OIDP Conference 2026 Call for Proposals on Participatory Democracy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Transformative OIDP Conference 2026 Call for Proposals presents a powerful opportunity for academics, policymakers, civil society leaders, and practitioners to contribute to one of the most important global discussions on the future of democracy. Organized by the International Observatory on Participatory Democracy (OIDP) in collaboration with the Municipality of Kraków and United Cities and Local Governments (UCLG), this landmark conference will take place in Kraków, Poland, from 21–23 September 2026.

Held under the theme “Hope and Renewal in the Face of a Global Crisis of Democracy – The Future of Participatory Democracy from Local to International Governance,” the conference seeks to address pressing global challenges affecting democratic systems and citizen participation worldwide. With the official deadline for proposal submissions set for April 30, 2026 (11:59 p.m. CEST), this is a timely opportunity for individuals and organizations to share insights, innovations, and practical solutions.

Transformative Global Dialogue on Participatory Democracy

The OIDP Conference is a globally recognized platform that convenes political leaders, public officials, researchers, activists, and practitioners to explore participatory democracy in action. As the 25th anniversary edition, the 2026 conference carries added significance, aiming not only to reflect on past achievements but also to chart the future of citizen engagement in governance.

In a world increasingly shaped by crises such as climate change, migration, geopolitical instability, and rising social polarization, democratic institutions face growing challenges. Declining trust in governance, the spread of misinformation, and the emergence of populist narratives have further complicated the democratic landscape. The Transformative OIDP Conference 2026 seeks to respond to these realities by fostering meaningful dialogue and collaboration across sectors and regions.

Transformative Themes Shaping the Conference

Participants are invited to submit proposals aligned with the conference’s thematic pathways, each designed to address critical dimensions of participatory democracy:

Transformative Citizen Participation in Times of Crisis

This theme explores how democratic systems can rebuild trust and strengthen civic engagement during periods of instability. It focuses on inclusion, resilience, and the role of citizens in navigating crises.

Transformative Deeper Democracy and Deliberation

This pathway examines how institutionalized deliberation processes influence representative democracy and enhance decision-making at various governance levels.

Transformative Participation in Democratic Transitions

This theme addresses the role of citizen engagement in shaping democratic structures during political and societal transformations.

Transformative Local Governments and Citizen Participation

Recognizing that democracy often begins at the local level, this pathway highlights the importance of local governance in fostering participatory practices and scaling them to national and international levels.

Types of Proposals Accepted

The conference encourages diverse and innovative contributions, welcoming both experienced speakers and first-time participants. Applicants may submit proposals in the following formats:

• Individual presentations (typically up to 15 minutes)

• Fully-formed panels (including 3–5 participants and a chair)

• Workshops or interactive sessions

• Project showcases demonstrating participatory practices

Special emphasis is placed on interactive and participatory formats that engage audiences actively, such as simulations, collaborative discussions, and experiential learning sessions.

Transformative Conference Experience in Kraków

The conference will be held in person in Kraków, Poland, offering a dynamic environment for networking, knowledge exchange, and collaboration. Participants will engage with a global community of stakeholders working to advance participatory democracy.

Key highlights of the conference include:

• Adoption of a Political Declaration outlining shared commitments

• OIDP Award Ceremony recognizing best practices in citizen participation

• Opportunities to build partnerships across governments, academia, and civil society

• Exposure to innovative participatory tools and methodologies

The working languages of the conference are English, Spanish, French, and Polish, with translation services provided during sessions.

It is important to note that while participation in the conference is free of charge, attendees are responsible for their own travel, accommodation, and related expenses.

Transformative Impact and Global Collaboration

Beyond presentations and discussions, the Transformative OIDP Conference 2026 aims to strengthen global alliances and foster long-term collaboration. By bringing together diverse perspectives and experiences, the conference seeks to generate actionable insights that can shape democratic practices worldwide.

Participants will have the opportunity to contribute to meaningful conversations that influence policy, strengthen civic engagement, and promote inclusive governance. The emphasis on both theoretical exploration and practical application ensures that outcomes are not only insightful but also impactful.

Application Process and Timeline

Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the conference concept note before submitting their proposals. Submissions must align with one of the thematic pathways and clearly demonstrate relevance, innovation, and potential impact.

Key Dates:

• Application Deadline: April 30, 2026 (11:59 p.m. CEST)

• Notification of Results: End of May 2026

• Conference Dates: September 21–23, 2026

Proposals will be reviewed by the Conference Scientific Committee, composed of experts and practitioners in participatory democracy. Selection criteria include academic and practical relevance, feasibility, clarity of ideas, and ability to engage diverse audiences.

Learn More About This Opportunity

To learn more about the Transformative OIDP Conference 2026 Call for Proposals and submit your proposal, click here

Conclusion

The Transformative OIDP Conference 2026 Call for Proposals is more than just an academic or professional opportunity—it is a platform to shape the future of democracy. By contributing your ideas, experiences, and innovations, you become part of a global movement dedicated to strengthening participatory governance and rebuilding trust in democratic systems.

For more conference opportunities on OFY Opportunities click here

The post Powerful OIDP Conference 2026 Call for Proposals on Participatory Democracy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Deep Learning Indaba 2026 Call for Papers: Publish AI Research in Africa and Join the Global Innovation Movement
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
Artificial Intelligence is shaping the future of the world—but Africa is not just participating; it is actively building that future. The Deep Learning Indaba 2026 Call for Papers is now open, offering researchers a unique opportunity to contribute to globally recognized AI research while addressing Africa’s most pressing challenges and opportunities. With a bold theme […]

The post Deep Learning Indaba 2026 Call for Papers: Publish AI Research in Africa and Join the Global Innovation Movement appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Artificial Intelligence is shaping the future of the world—but Africa is not just participating; it is actively building that future. The Deep Learning Indaba 2026 Call for Papers is now open, offering researchers a unique opportunity to contribute to globally recognized AI research while addressing Africa’s most pressing challenges and opportunities.

With a bold theme centered on “Sovereign Intelligence. African Scale,” the 2026 edition aims to position African researchers at the forefront of AI innovation, ownership, and development.

The deadline for abstract submissions is April 15, 2026, and full papers must be submitted by April 20, 2026.

What is Deep Learning Indaba?

Deep Learning Indaba is a leading pan-African movement dedicated to strengthening machine learning and artificial intelligence across the continent.

It brings together:

  • Students
  • Academic researchers
  • Industry professionals
  • Entrepreneurs

Each year, the Indaba convenes over 1,000 participants from more than 45 African countries, creating a vibrant ecosystem for collaboration, innovation, and knowledge exchange.

The 2026 edition will take place in Nigeria, a nation known for its scale, creativity, and rapidly growing tech ecosystem.

What’s New in 2026: Expanded Research Track

For 2026, the Indaba is significantly expanding its Research Track to include:

  • Full-length, peer-reviewed papers
  • Indexed academic publications
  • Inclusion in a special volume of International Joint Conference on Artificial Intelligence (IJCAI)

Accepted papers will be presented at the Research in Africa Showcase, giving authors global visibility and recognition.

Theme 2026: Sovereign Intelligence. African Scale

The 2026 theme reflects a critical shift in global AI discourse: ownership, autonomy, and local relevance.

Sovereign Intelligence refers to Africa’s ability to:

  • Build its own AI systems
  • Control and manage its data
  • Define its scientific and technological direction

As global competition accelerates toward Artificial General Intelligence (AGI), this theme emphasizes Africa’s role not as a consumer, but as a creator and leader in AI innovation.

Who Should Submit?

This Call for Papers is open to researchers working across core AI domains, especially those focusing on:

  • Machine learning
  • Data science
  • Natural language processing
  • Computer vision
  • AI applications in African contexts

Key Requirement:

At least one author must be an Africa-based African researcher, ensuring that research remains grounded in local realities and expertise.

Benefits of Participation

Submitting to the Deep Learning Indaba offers multiple academic and professional advantages:

1. Global Publication Opportunity

Accepted papers will be published in an internationally recognized IJCAI volume.

2. Conference Presentation

Authors will present their work at the prestigious Research in Africa Showcase.

3. Complimentary Conference Pass

One author per accepted paper will receive a free conference pass, with priority given to Africa-based researchers.

4. Networking and Collaboration

Participants gain access to a diverse network of AI experts, institutions, and innovators across Africa and beyond.

Key Deadlines

  • Abstract Submission Deadline: April 15, 2026
  • Full Paper Submission Deadline: April 20, 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare early and ensure their submissions meet academic standards.

Why This Opportunity Matters

The Deep Learning Indaba is more than a conference it is a movement.

It represents a collective commitment to:

  • Elevate African AI research globally
  • Foster inclusive innovation ecosystems
  • Build capacity across the continent
  • Ensure Africa plays a central role in shaping the future of AI

Inspired by the words of Wole Soyinka,
“A tiger does not proclaim its tigritude; it pounces,”
the Indaba embodies action, excellence, and impact.

How to Participate

Interested researchers can submit their abstracts and full papers through the official Deep Learning Indaba submission platform.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Deep Learning Indaba 2026 Call for Papers: Publish AI Research in Africa and Join the Global Innovation Movement appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
